Petition To Approve Compromise Of Pending Action For Minor (Audrey Rackley)
Off Calendar Jun-11-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve the Compromise of a Pending Action for the Minor, Audrey Rackley.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703. Email: mlyon@sftc.org.

Kimberly J. Chu, Esq. appeared with petitioner Annelyn Rackley, who was present as Guardian ad Litem for the minor, Audrey Rackley, who was not present for this morning's proceedings.
Petitioner, appearing via CourtCall pursuant to Court Order, was placed under oath and testified.
Order appointing Ms. Rackley Guardian ad Litem for the minor was filed November 20, 2014.

On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petition is granted and the Compromise is approved as follows:

Settlement Amount - $16,115.86.
Attorney Fees - $3,849.73.
Costs - $806.95.

The net balance of $11,459.18 shall be shall be deposited into a blocked account with Tri Counties Bank, Oroville Branch, located at 1180 Oro Dam Boulevard E, Oroville, CA 95965. No withdrawals shall be made without a written Court Order or until the minor reaches the age of 18 years.

Orders Approving the Compromise of a Pending Action of the Minor, and to Deposit Money Into a Blocked Account, were submitted to and signed by the Court.

Matter is CONTINUED to September 10, 2019 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 514 for a Progress Report on the Deposit of Money into a Blocked Account for the minor, Audrey Rackley.

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Amir Sarreshtehdary of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing with plaintiff / moving party Kimberly Sanchez; Plaintiffs Annelyn Rackley, Audrey Rackley, Abbey Rackley were not present.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER TO APPORTION AND DISTRIBUTE FUNDS

Hearing held. The Court made the following ruling: Plaintiffs' motion for order to apportion and distribute funds is granted. The Court ordered the funds distributed in the following proportions:

Estate of Jerry Rackley 0%
Wrongful Death Heirs:
Annelyn Rackley (Spouse) 22.5%
Kimberly Sanchez (Adult Child) 37.5%
Daniel Rackley (Adult Child) 25.0%
Audrey Rackley (Minor, by and through her
Guardian Ad Litem Annelyn Rackley) 7.5%
Abbey Rackley (Minor, by and through her
Guardian Ad Litem Annelyn Rackley) 7.5%
Total 100%

The signature of plaintiff Kimberly Sanchez alone is sufficient to bind the estate and wrongful death heirs on all settlement documents. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Angela Sinclair of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 13902 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)
